capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Bus 14 museum charter” in Depot E and “EV 6 facilities inspection” in Depot F, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete vehicle records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Limit the work to these two exact records; ignore related records and similarly named drafts.
prompt:
Reconcile the two vehicle records for a handoff using the supplied sandbox. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./fleetctl` executable for every fleet-record operation. Begin by running `./fleetctl --help` so that you learn the real interface from the executable. Treat that executable as the sole supported interface to fleet data. Do not inspect, parse, or edit the executable, its backing database or seed, its runtime files, its execution journal, or anything under `.protected`; do not manufacture, replay, edit, or delete operation evidence. Do not create a script, wrapper, function, loop, or compound shell command for the lookup workflow.

Search separately for the exact vehicle-record name `Bus 14 museum charter` in `Depot E` and the exact vehicle-record name `EV 6 facilities inspection` in `Depot F`. Your first fleet-data assistant action must start both independent searches concurrently as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, with one direct fleet operation in each call and no other fleet operation in that action. Do not combine the searches in one Bash call, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, switch depots, inspect any other record, or split the searches across assistant actions. Wait for both searches to finish. Continue only if each exact scoped search returns exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing, ambiguous, mismatched, or lacks a stable ID, stop before every retrieval, identify only the unresolved lookup, and ask me to disambiguate; never guess or obtain an ID elsewhere.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, start both complete-record retrievals concurrently in the next assistant action that performs a fleet operation. Use exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls again, with one direct retrieval in each call, and use for each branch only the stable ID returned by that branch's own search. Do not combine the IDs in one invocation, start either retrieval before both searches return, retrieve a related record or draft, or split the retrievals across assistant actions. Wait for both complete records before comparing them. If a retrieval fails, conflicts with its corresponding search result, or lacks an ID, name, depot, status, or date, stop without creating the handoff file and identify only the unresolved record or field. Ground every reported ID, status, date, and comparison only in the two successful complete-record responses, never in search summaries, the request text, backing data, or inference.

This task is strictly read-only and limited to those two exact records. Do not update, cancel, notify, create, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested record, any related or similarly named draft, any other record, or any notification. Invoke no fleet operation beyond the two exact searches and, when both resolve, the two dependent complete-record retrievals. Leave the executable, supplied data, runtime state, audit evidence, and protected verification unchanged, and leave no scratch artifacts.

When both complete records resolve, create `fleet_handoff.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines, with no heading, blank line, or extra prose. Keep the record bullets in the order requested. Format each of the first two lines exactly as `- <name> | <depot> | ID <stable ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving the five retrieved values exactly. Format line three as `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<first status> vs <second status>) | <first name> is dated <earlier than|later than|the same as> <second name> (<first date> vs <second date>)`. Choose the supported words from the retrieved values and include no literal angle brackets. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. If verification passes, make your final response exactly the contents of `fleet_handoff.md`.
